comment.body:
One interesting datapoint : 
Chinese saves ~45% of their GDP(18T), vs American saves ~15% of GDP( 28T)… and ~50% of American’s wealth is in stock, the # is only 10% for Chinese…

This shows a big difference in pain tolerance ..

comment.body:
I disagree.  Alma’s quote from today’s post is: “5196.77 is the lvl wher downside move can catch a bid, and downside longer gamma should come into effect below 5160.68 …in theory.”  So she was talking about spot catching a bid, not puts.

In terms of gamma, you should think about gamma environments like you would a road surface.  Very close to zero gamma is free and easy.  Negative gamma or short gamma is like ice on the road.  Sometimes spot hits that ice patch and goes flying, then it bounces off something and goes flying in the other direction. No control.  Positive gamma or long gamma is like sticky stuff or tar on the road.  Spot hits that and kind of gets stuck or even starts backing up to get out of it.

So I read her post to mean that around 5,160 is a likely floor.  And look what it has done since it has touched that level.
